title: Project support officers
description: Analyze and coordinate the ongoing logistical functions of a firm or organization. Responsible for the entire life cycle of a product, including acquisition, distribution, internal allocation, delivery, and final disposal of resources. Design or analyze operational solutions for projects such as transportation optimization, network modeling, process and methods analysis, cost containment, capacity enhancement, routing and shipment optimization, or information management. Analyze product delivery or supply chain processes to identify or recommend changes. May manage route activity including invoicing, electronic bills, and shipment tracing. (Based on 13 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Write computer programs or applications in programming languages to conduct analyses and manage data.
Organize and code data prior to computer entry, ensuring accuracy and preparing it for analysis.
Prepare tables, graphs, and reports summarizing research results, ensuring clarity and thoroughness.
Conduct internet-based and library research, optimizing processes for project completion.
Verify the accuracy of data entered in databases and correct errors when necessary.
Develop and implement project-specific data management plans that address coding, reporting, and workflow processes.
Analyze logistics and operational data using methods such as data mining and cost-benefit analysis.
Confer with project stakeholders to identify data needs, manage requirements, and implement solutions.
Prepare and execute logistical strategies for optimizing supply chains and distribution processes.
Maintain and update logistics records and databases in accordance with organizational policies.
Monitor inventory transactions to assess storage, shipping, and inventory integrity within facilities.
Provide technical support for existing reports and dashboards to aid decision-making processes.
Train project staff on technical procedures, data management, and software applications.
Evaluate processes and technologies related to logistics and suggest revisions to enhance efficiency.
Create or review technical documentation to ensure accurate data reporting and management.
Monitor environmental impacts of logistics activities and develop strategies for mitigation.
Participate in the preparation of project-related reports, ensuring compliance with design and technical specifications.
Manage project activities, including hiring staff and overseeing operational needs.
Identify and analyze trends in logistics or industry data to support strategic planning.
Prepare logistic strategies and capacity analyses for distribution or transportation functions.
Communicate with customers and suppliers to improve service levels and operational efficiency.
Stay informed of logistics technology advances to enhance operational effectiveness.
Direct and support the compilation and analysis of technical source data necessary for product development.
Report project plans, progress, and results; track product flow from origin to final delivery.
Direct team activities, establishing task priorities, scheduling and tracking work assignments, and ensuring resource availability.
Develop and implement technical project management tools, such as plans, schedules, and compliance matrices.
Review logistics performance with customers against targets and service agreements, and propose logistics solutions.
Collaborate with departments to meet customer requirements, minimize negative impacts during shortages, and develop an understanding of their needs.
Perform system lifecycle cost analysis, develop component studies, and maintain cost estimates or forecasts.
Manage the logistical aspects of product life cycles, including the distribution and allocation of materials, and minimizing obsolescence.
Prepare manuals and train workers in new procedures or systems, ensuring effective use according to organizational policy.
Analyze data gathered and develop solutions or alternative methods of proceeding, while conducting logistics studies and analyses.
Gather and organize information on problems or procedures, and document findings of studies with recommendations.
Maintain and develop positive relationships with key customer personnel involved in logistics activities.
Monitor industry standards and trends to identify developments in logistics, analyzing processes for efficiency or cost reduction.
Prepare reports on logistics performance measures and provide ongoing analyses in areas such as transportation costs and delivery processes.
Review forms and reports with management and users to identify problems and improvements, and enhance existing logistics processes.
Type, format, and proofread documents and correspondence, while maintaining inventory and ordering materials and supplies.
Communicate and coordinate with various suppliers, carriers, and contractors to address inquiries and ensure operational efficiency.
Design and evaluate distribution centers, and develop specifications for equipment and facility layouts.
Review contractual commitments and specifications to determine logistics requirements and streamline operating processes.
Enter logistics-related data into databases and review documentation for accuracy, including vendor payments and purchasing files.
